name: BlueTrane Group, LLC  DBA BlueTrane Heritage Farm
notes: We specialize in the preservation and promotion of the endangered heritage breeds and heirloom varieties. Our produce is pesticide, fungicide and herbicide free. All are naturally grown in a sustaining way consistent with organic principles.

name: Castle Rock Farmers Market
notes: The Castle Rock Farmers Market has provided Douglas County residents with a local market where they can purchase fresh produce and artisan made food and goods for over 30 years.

name: City of Loveland Parks & Rec
notes: The Loveland Farmers Market provides local produce, meat, dairy, treats, pantry foods and artisan goods Sundays June-September from 9A-1P. Additional special event days throughout the season and artist markets the last Sunday of each month.

name: Country Road Vines and Wines
notes: A boutique winery located on the great plains in Northeast Colorado.  All wines are produced and bottled at the winery.  Wine flights, glasses and bottles of wine are available to enjoy in the tasting room or on the patio.  Take a moment to experience paradise on the plains!

name: Creek Side Gardens
notes: family owned & operated retail garden center, greenhouse & nursery

name: Crested Butte Farmers Market
notes: The CB Farmers Market is dedicated to promoting sustainable agriculture.  We offer organic and naturally grown vegetables, fruits, herbs, pasture-raised and grass-finished meats, artisanal cheese, wine, hard ciders & juices, breads, jams, pickles, cut flowers and a local arts and crafts.

name: Davis Family Livestock
notes: Davis Family Livestock offers educational tours to view three breeds of sheep including the rare Valais Blacknose, voted the "World's Cutest Sheep", alpacas, llama, horses and chickens.  Visitors also learn about wool, wool processing and visit the gift store for fiber products and homemade jams and jellies.

name: DeVries Fruit & Veggie Roadside Stand

DeVries Fruit & Veggies (Since 1943).  Located North of Montrose on Highway 50 between Mile Markers 85 & 86.

name: Downtown Grand Junction Market on Main
notes: Parking available along side streets and in the 5th & Rood parking garage. Parking is free after 4 p.m. All products are made or grown in the local region. We welcome SNAP, and Double Up Food Bucks.

name: Elevation Mountain Grown Herbal Tea, LLC
notes: Elevation Mountain Grown Herbal Tea is a woman-owned, home-grown, farm-to-teacup business. Every tea creation step happens onsite. Visit our cultivation fields. Take a tour and see how we wash, dry, grind, blend and package our teas. Pick your own berries, herbs and flowers. Sample the tea. Browse our gift shop.

name: Ent Wise Farm
notes: No pesticides, No Herbicides, No fungicides of any kind. We are growing into our dream of running a wholistic and ecologically minded model. Come say hello and see! Volunteer or share our outdoor coworking space.

name: Evergreen Farmers Market THE ORIGINAL

name: Fort Morgan Farmers Market at The Block
notes: The Fort Morgan Farmers Market is back for it's fourth season at The Block. Experience and celebrate northeastern Colorado's agricultural roots with fresh produce, flowers, locally crafted items and more!

name: Lafayette Colorado Farmers Market
notes: The Lafayette Farmers Market in Colorado operates every Sunday from May until October, from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. in downtown Lafayette. It features a growers-only selection from local farmers, ranchers, and artisans, offering fresh produce, meats, dairy, and prepared foods.

notes: Lazy Acres Ranch raises small batches of Berkshire pork, chicken and rabbit using regenerative principles and organic feed. Our goal is to manage the animals so they are happy, healthy and their impact improves soil health all while producing nutrient dense food for our family and yours.

name: Legacy Meats
notes: Local beef and pork products available for single purchases or bulk buy options.

name: Long's Gardens

name: Loveland West Farmers Market at JAX
notes: The market is open from April 22nd to November 3rd and is located in the JAX West parking lot, where plenty of parking is available. Rain or shine, stop by to enjoy breakfast or lunch while exploring our offerings. Don't miss our monthly specials! Plus, during summer break, we host a kids' program.

name: Rehfeld Ranch Raised

notes: The City Park Farmers Market is a growers-focused market that runs on Saturdays (Mayâ€“October) at City Park in Denver. With 100+ local vendors, fresh produce, meats, dairy, baked goods, local packaged food, and prepared food. It accepts SNAP and DUFB.

notes: The Montrose Farmers Market is a year-round market featuring farmers, ranchers, producers, and artisans of the Western Slope. Guidestone's farmstand is open Tuesdays 4-6pm, May-October.

We research and publish on bees with CU and CSU.

notes: Several years ago, some simple Benedictine Fathers had a dream. Today, that dream is a reality, and the staff at the winery proudly invites you to share in their vision.

name: SALT Craft Meat Market
hours: Mon: 9:30am - 6pm; Tue: 9:30am - 6pm; Wed: 9:30am - 6pm; Thu: 9:30am - 6pm; Fri: 9:30am - 6pm; Sat: 9am - 5pm
notes: We are a local craft butcher shop, offering high quality, locally-raised meats and grab & go options like made-from-scratch meals and deli sandwiches. We exist to feed & nourish the community. We are the local choice for the best meats in Castle Rock, connecting the community and local farms and ranches.

name: Larimer County Farmers' Market
notes: The oldest farmers' market in northern Colorado, the Larimer County Farmers' Market is celebrating its 50th season in 2026, with over 100 vendors. The LCFM is Fort Collins' flagship market, and operated by the Larimer County CSU Extension Office.

name: Billy Goat Hop Farm
hours: Mon: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Tue: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Wed: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Thu: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Fri: Fridays on the Farm 4pm - 9pm / By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Sat: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs; Sun: By appointment for tours / Campground 24 hrs
notes: We are now the only hop farm in the Southwest, and are now open to the public with camping, tours, and events! This is a unique opportunity to learn about hop farming, sleep among hop bines, enjoy mountain views, and drink beer & hop water made with hops grown right here!

name: Pfaltzgraff Farms
notes: Come visit a regenerative farm where we grow sunflowers, millet, heirloom corn, black-eyed peas, and more! We have a dedicated millhouse where we mill our own grains for gluten-free flours and baking mixes. Give us a call for a tour or follow us on social media for the next crop bloom!!

name: Rifle Farmers Market
notes: We are the Rifle Farmers Market. A Friday night gathering space with local produce, eggs, honey and handmade products. We have live music, food trucks and beer and wine to be enjoyed onsite.

name: Lyons Farmers Market
notes: The Lyons Farmers Market is a curated Sunday market celebrating local food and seasonal craftsmanship. It is a living cultural space where food, land, creativity, and community converge. The 2026 Lyons Farmers Market will be held every Sunday 9a-1p in Sandstone Park, from May 10th - October 4th.

name: Green Junction Farmstead
notes: We are Colorado Proud and living soil growers. We don't use any chemicals on our land or produce. We are growing the way nature intended.

name: Nederland Farmers Market
notes: A vibrant mountain community market featuring fresh local produce, pasture-raised meats, baked goods, handmade crafts, and live music in the heart of Nederland.

name: Black Mountain Bison
notes: Black Mountain Bison offers premium American bison meat, sourced locally and available for local pick up and delivery. Our range includes a variety of cuts, from tender steaks to flavorful roasts. With no minimum order requirements, customers can enjoy the f inest bison meat with convenience and quality guaranteed.

name: Bartels Land & Livestock Inc.
notes: 26th annual pumpkin patch. No admission to the patch you pay for the product you pick from the field or the stand. Food trucks, Indian corn, straw bales , farm animals, lots and lots of pumpkins , gourds . We also offer birthday parties during our fall activities. Parties are done by appointment.

name: Larry Zierer
products: We offer sales of alpaca related products (yarns
blankets and socks) as well as the alpacas themselves
notes: We have been raising alpacas for over 25 years and always welcome farm visits to educate people about these amazing animals and to help them get started in the alpaca business.  We currently have a herd of over 30 alpacas with several crias(baby alpacas) due this spring and fall.

name: Longmont Farmers Market

name: Boulder Farmers Market
notes: Join us at the Boulder Farmers Market! Here you'll meet your local farmers, ranchers, beekeepers, and entrepreneurs selling everything from fresh produce, meat, cheese, fresh bread and pastries, as well as delicious ready-to-eat options for breakfast and lunch.

name: Sunflower Farm Acres
hours: Wed: 12:30-4:30; Thu: 9-4:30; Fri: 9-4:30; Sat: Depends on Season, 11-3 in April & 9-1:00 from May-October; Sun: Depends on Season, 11-3 in April & 9-1:00 from May-OctoberSeason
notes: Sunflower Farm Acres is a vegetable and flower farm entering its third year of production. Located on Prospect Road in southern Longmont, we integrate regenerative farming practices to grow organic and nourishing foods for the community.

name: Arriola Sunshine Farm
notes: We offer registered Navajo-Churro sheep breeding stock, grass fed lamb, wool and pelts raised humanely with regenerative agricultural practices.

name: Podunk's Ranch
notes: Podunkâ€™s Ranch in Cortez, Colorado provides grass-fed, grain-finished beef sold directly from our ranch to your table. Shop individual cuts, bundles, or whole, half, and quarter beef. Online store, local markets, and ranch pick-up available. Monthly beef CSA offered. SNAP accepted to support access to local food. BQA Cert.

name: Sambrito Creek Farm
notes: First-generation family farm raising 100% grass-fed Icelandic sheep. As proud members of The Good Meat Project, we prioritize ethical stewardship and transparency. We offer premium breeding stock, live shares, and select USDA-packaged meat for those seeking hardy heritage genetics and high-quality, sustainably raised lamb.

name: Native Hill Farm
hours: Mon: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Tue: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Wed: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Thu: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Fri: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Sat: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.; Sun: 9 a.m. - 6 p.m.
notes: We are honored to be growing organic, fresh food for our community. Our produce is available through our Farm Share programs, the Larimer County Farmerâ€™s Market, and at our onsite Farm Stand. You can also find our high quality produce on the menu at select Old Town Fort Collins restaurants.

name: New Roots Farm LLC
notes: We recognize that healthy, living soil is the cornerstone of the entire farm ecosystem. Healthy soil means healthy plants, healthy people and animals, and healthy planet. Here at New Roots Farm in the Arkansas River Valley, we gratefully and humbly care for the living soil that feeds us.

notes: Our Red Barn Ranch is on 40 acres of rolling prairie. We are located 2 miles south of the town of Calhan, northeast of Colorado Springs. We are about two miles from The Paint Mine Interpretive Park. We also raise Colorado Mountain Dogs. They have full reign of the property, so please leave your pets at home.

name: Sprout City Farms
notes: Sprout City Farms is an urban agriculture nonprofit (founded 2010) with 2 community farms in Denver & Lakewood. Half of our harvest is dedicated to food access programs for low-income families. Educational/community programs include Intern/Apprenticeships, Volunteer Days, Workshops, Tours, Events, and more--see our website!

name: DeLaney Community Farm - by Project Worthmore
notes: DeLaney Community Farm is a 5-acre farm managed by nonprofit Project Worthmore supporting refugees through sustainable agriculture and community building. Our goal is to provide the space, support, resources, and expertise for refugees to grow healthy, organically-produced food for themselves and their broader community.

notes: Sandy's Way Microfarm grows Certified Naturally Grown vegetables in Sedalia, Colorado. Enjoy free delivery, 7 pickup locations, and Harvest Membership benefits with 10% discounts.

name: Chinampas
products: Depends on what we are powering our system with at the time
notes: Chinampas is a Southwest Colorado farm focused on growing fresh, high-quality mushrooms year-round. We pair mushroom cultivation with aquaponics to produce clean, nutrient-dense food using minimal waste. Our goal is to strengthen the local food system with reliable, sustainably grown products.

name: Switch Gears Farm
hours: Sat: 8AM - 1PM (City Park Farmers Market); Sun: 9AM - 1PM (Lafayette Farmers Market)
notes: Switch Gears Farm is a small-scale farm, primarily focused on high-quality produce and soil health - we do not use pesticides or synthetic fertilizers. You can find us at farmers markets or send an email - we do not have a farmstand at the farm. We also sell to chefs, small retailers, non-profits, and schools.

name: Rio Grande Organic Growers COOP
notes: The Rio Grande Growers are a farmer cooperative that cultivates about 5 acres located at the Rio Grande Farm Park in Alamosa, CO.  We do not use any pesticides or chemicals on our plants, only natural organic compost and weed by hand in small acre plots. From July to September we sell fresh produce and herbs to markets.

name: Tuff's Ranch

name: Louisville Farmers Market
notes: Weekly farmers market featuring Colorado-grown fruits, vegetables, eggs, and meat. Live music, dog friendly and plenty of ready to consume breakfast and lunch offerings.

name: Salida Farmers Market
notes: Salidaâ€™s Farmers Market takes place at Alpine Park, a centrally located 2-acre park adjacent to Salidaâ€™s vibrant downtown. Filled with grass and trees, Alpine Park provides an easy and ideal retreat. This community-focused event invites you to fill your basket with fresh local food and connect with the community!

name: Gunnison Farmers Market
notes: Seasonal farmers market in downtown Gunnison featuring an array of produce, meats, alcoholic and non alcoholic beverages, fresh baked goods including sourdough bread, jewelry, photography, clothing, and other artisan goods. Live music in IOOF Park.

name: The Farm Collaborative
notes: The Farm Co Market at the Farm Collaborative features regenerative produce sourced within 100 miles, complemented by our booth at the Aspen Farmers Market. Beyond retail, we empower our community through farmer training, tours, and the 2Forks Club, which provides critical 0% interest loans to local producers.

name: Antelope Creek Ranch Wagyu
notes: Antelope Creek Ranch Wagyu is a small family-owned Wagyu Beef Ranch in Wiggins, Colorado, specializing in Full-Blood Japanese Wagyu Genetics.

name: Alamosa Farmers' Market
notes: The Alamosa Farmers' Market is the San Luis Valley's largest and oldest market.  A Downtown community event on Saturdays from July through October we offer local foods, hand crafted goods, live music, activities for kids, and community engagement opportunities.  An affordable market with Valley flavor.

name: Dillon Farmers Market
notes: Join us Fridays from June 5 through September 25, as 100+ vendors offer Colorado produce, cheeses, treats, gifts and more at the Dillon Farmers Market. Live music and food trucks add to a fun atmosphere for all ages.

name: Hazel Dell Mushrooms
notes: Hazel Dell is Colorado's premier exotic mushroom grower. Established in 1997, the farm is family owned and operated and produces the highest quality certified organic culinary mushrooms. The farm store offers up to 12 different varieties of fresh, dried and powdered mushrooms and inspired goodies.

name: Mutual Dream Farm
notes: Mutual Dream Farm, nestled in Bailey, Colorado, is committed to providing sustainably raised, nutritious food and natural, homemade goods while enhancing the environment through soil-building permaculture practices. We offer a variety of farm-fresh products, including chicken eggs, goat meat, lamb,

name: Orchard Farmers MArket
notes: The Orchard Farmers Market features 50+ market vendors each Sunday in the Macy's parking lot at the Orchard Town Center off 144th and I25. Join us for produce, food items, baked goods, beverages, crafts and more!

name: McWiggly Worm Farm LLC
products: worm castings and worm casting tea/extract
notes: We are a local, home-based worm farm. We raise and sell composting and fishing worms. We also sell worm castings and worm casting tea/extract. Worm castings and worm casting tea are one of the best, all natural soil inputs for anything with roots.

notes: Family-owned and operated in Strasburg, Wolf Creek Fresh is dedicated to providing 100% natural, free-range, and regeneratively farmed food. We offer a variety of fresh seasonal produce, free-range eggs, and specialty items. We can accept Snap/EBT benefits at the Bennett Farmersâ€™ Market only. Visit our website.

name: Padilla Prouce Inc.
notes: Padilla Produce Inc. is a family-owned farm market with locations in Manzanola and Colorado Springs, offering wholesale and direct-to-consumer fresh produce through farmers markets, including watermelons, cantaloupe, peppers, onions, and alfalfa. We provide affordable, high-quality produce.

name: Pope Farms Produce & Garden Center LLC
hours: Mon: 9 am-6 pm; Tue: 9 am-6 pm; Wed: 9 am-6 pm; Thu: 9 am-6 pm; Fri: 9 am-6 pm; Sat: 9 am-6 pm; Sun: 10 am - 4pm

name: Bennett Summer Pop-up Market
notes: This small pop-up market aims to increase local access to produce grown in Colorado, in particular for farmers and food producers along the I-70 corridor! You'll find us in the parking lot behind the Muegge House.

name: Yampa Valley Foods
notes: Yampa Valley Foods is a nonprofit marketplace that brings locally raised, grown, and produced goods to the people of the Yampa Valley. We offer a year-round selection of beef, pork, lamb, chicken, eggs, honey, cheese, seasonal veggies, and more and work with over 80 local producers.
